column1: (***Update: My son at FSU just told me classes are cancelled on Monday) For folks with kids at FSU or FAMU, you're probably gonna want to keep an eye on this storm. While the official forecast barely has it becoming a hurricane at landfall, there is a ton of warm water and energy out there and it is certainly possible it could be stronger. Could be close enough to Tally for impacts. Strong Tropical Storm force winds and some power outages on Monday. Lots of time to update but keep your kids in the loop. We'll update 24/7 as always.
FSU has already suspended classes until Tuesday.
I flew my son home to Chicago yesterday from FAMU because of this storm. In two years he's had two Hurricanes threats, a tornado and now this.
Quite a welcoming to Florida!
exactly! It's suppose to be sunshine and blue skies! An emergency evacuation plan was required for admissions. I should've known then. 🤨
and 98% of the time you don’t need an evac plan but in Chicago you need thunderstorm, tornado and blizzard plans!
we don't make plans for any of those things. We go to work and school during blizzards and thunderstorms.
Welcome to Florida 😉

Suggestion - follow Dennis Phillips, Weather man. He explains things so anyone can understand and hes pretty accurate. Hes got a following and you can find him on line. Its great for us Chicago ppl who dont know a lot about FL weather and how Hurricanes work.
